author: Tertullian
book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
As the Flesh is a Partaker with the Soul in All Human Conduct, So Will It Be in the Recompense of Eternity.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: As the Flesh is a Partaker with the Soul in All Human Conduct, So Will It Be in the Recompense of Eternity.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Come now, let our opponents sever the connection of the flesh with the soul in the affairs of life, that they may be emboldened to sunder it also in the recompense of life.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Let them deny their association in acts, that they may be fairly able to deny also their participation in rewards.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The flesh ought not to have any share in the sentence, if it had none in the cause of it.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Let the soul alone be called back, if it alone went away.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But (nothing of the kind ever happened); for the soul alone no more departed from life, than it ran through alone the course from which it departed—I mean this present life.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Indeed, the soul alone is so far from conducting (the affairs of) life, that we do not withdraw from community with the flesh even our thoughts, however isolated they be, however unprecipitated into act by means of the flesh; since whatever is done in man’s heart is done by the soul in the flesh, and with the flesh, and through the flesh.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The Lord Himself, in short, when rebuking our thoughts, includes in His censures this aspect of the flesh, (man’s heart), the citadel of the soul: “Why think ye evil in your hearts?” and again: “Whosoever looketh on a woman, to lust after her, hath already committed adultery with her in his heart.”

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: So that even the thought, without operation and without effect, is an act of the flesh.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But if you allow that the faculty which rules the senses, and which they call Hegemonikon, has its sanctuary in the brain, or in the interval between the eyebrows, or wheresoever the philosophers are pleased to locate it, the flesh will still be the thinking place of the soul.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: The soul is never without the flesh, as long as it is in the flesh.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: There is nothing which the flesh does not transact in company with the soul, when without it it does not exist.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Consider carefully, too, whether the thoughts are not administered by the flesh, since it is through the flesh that they are distinguished and known externally.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Let the soul only meditate some design, the face gives the indication—the face being the mirror of all our intentions.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: They may deny all combination in acts, but they cannot gainsay their co-operation in thoughts.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Still they enumerate the sins of the flesh; surely, then, for its sinful conduct it must be consigned to punishment.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: But we, moreover, allege against them the virtues of the flesh; surely also for its virtuous conduct it deserves a future reward.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Again, as it is the soul which acts and impels us in all we do, so it is the function of the flesh to render obedience.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Now we are not permitted to suppose that God is either unjust or idle.

verses.book: On the Resurrection of the Flesh.
verses.text: Unjust, (however He would be,) were He to exclude from reward the flesh which is associated in good works; and idle, were He to exempt it from punishment, when it has been an accomplice in evil deeds: whereas human judgment is deemed to be the more perfect, when it discovers the agents in every deed, and neither spares the guilty nor grudges the virtuous their full share of either punishment or praise with the principals who employed their services.
